seoTitle: Email Multiple Invoices at Once From QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: Send several customer invoices in one batch using QuickBooks Online's filter and batch action tools instead of emailing them one by one.
intro: Sending invoices one at a time wastes time when you have a stack of them ready to go out. This guide shows how to filter, select, and batch-email multiple invoices at once in QuickBooks Online.

steps.heading: Open the Invoices grid
steps.body: Go to Sales, then Invoices, to see your full list of billing documents.

steps.heading: Filter for pending invoices
steps.body: Use the Status filter to isolate invoices marked Needs Sending or Unsent.

steps.heading: Select multiple invoices
steps.body: Check the master checkbox to select all visible rows, or manually uncheck any you want to exclude.

steps.heading: Trigger the batch action
steps.body: Click the Batch actions dropdown at the top of the table and choose Send or Email.

steps.heading: Confirm and send
steps.body: Review the recipient summary in the confirmation pop-up, then click Send to deliver all selected invoices at once.

faq.question: Can I exclude specific invoices from a batch send?
faq.answer: Yes, after selecting all with the master checkbox, you can manually uncheck any invoices you want to leave out.

faq.question: Will batch sending resend invoices that were already emailed?
faq.answer: It depends on which invoices you select; filtering by Needs Sending or Unsent status helps avoid resending ones already delivered.

faq.question: How do I know the batch email actually went out?
faq.answer: Check your delivery logs or the invoice status column, which updates once the emails are successfully sent.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many invoices I can send at once?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online supports selecting many invoices for a batch send, though very large batches may take a moment to process.
